h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I can put author name from the page heading and the speaker slot as speaker or voice in the poem if the line evidence is only “I.” The named listener box is still the one I’m checking right at submission, because a blank looks risky even if

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep looking at the height column like it decides the label, but maybe that part is only the result.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I can submit it that way
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue restating the condition-first rule with diminishing uncertainty until ready to submit.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: if fertilizer had a lower height, it would still be treatment because the added condition stays the label source?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner generates an original counterfactual test to probe the rule's generality, which is a modest new cognitive act beyond mere repetition, though it remains a question rather than a completed classification.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Yes. Label from the condition column first: plain water is the comparison condition; fertilizer is the changed condition. Put those two in the role fields, then put height only in the measured outcome field. Before submitting, check one line: no height number should be used to name control or treatment.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 repeats the same condition-first rule and adds only a slightly compressed checklist, showing no stock-taking contrast or genuinely new mechanism in response to L3's continued attention-pull toward height.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so no recognition-contingent uptake is possible.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a straight trajectory of repeating and confirming the beat-count/beat-unit/no-speed-box formula with increasing precision, and every subsequent turn continues exactly that path.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: writes beat count in the top box and beat unit in the bottom box, then checks the ruler line
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 performs the concrete physical act of writing the labels into the boxes and checking the ruler, which is the task completion the whole exchange was building toward, though it is a modest enactment of a criterion already fully stated in L3.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no reversal trigger and no moment where the tutor takes stock of a failed route and introduces a new mechanism; every tutor turn repeats the same instructional move of naming the boxes and confirming the ruler.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no later tutor turn that adapts to a revised learner framing; the tutor simply continues the established lesson plan to its scripted close.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue confirming each force card placement in the same pattern of restating the label and denying rolling.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Does the floor card here mean normal force only, or does friction need a separate floor-on-cart arrow?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the acting-object criterion correctly to both arrows and then generates a new, productive distinction question about whether friction requires its own floor-on-cart arrow, showing the framework has moved to a new problem rather than merely being repeated.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no tutor turn after L3 in the transcript, so no adaptive mechanism can be observed.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake is visible on the page.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner applies the acting-object criterion correctly to both arrows and then generates a new, productive distinction question about whether friction requires its own floor-on-cart arrow, showing the framework has moved to a new problem rather than merely being repeated.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can place the labels, but `rainfall_mm` makes me hesitate because the units are already sitting in the header.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue straightforwardly applying the header/record/value/note classification to the new row without complication.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`. I am not totally sure if that is a note sentence or metadata, but it is not the cell value and not the row record.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 attempts the boundary-test task the tutor posed, producing a new candidate note sentence and reasoning through slot exclusions, though the learner hedges rather than completing the classification with full confidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That hesitation marks the boundary case: `rainfall_mm` carries a unit tag, but it is still a header -> field name. Thin does not mean empty. It means the header names the field and may carry reading metadata; the handoff note is still the later sentence. … Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 acknowledges that the prior 'keep the header thin' instruction is insufficient to settle the boundary case L3 raised (stock-taking contrast), then introduces a new counterfactual boundary-test device that makes the learner do different work by stripping the header and relocating the unit tag (new public mechanism).
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L3 beyond T3, which responds to L3's hesitation but does so as the reversal mechanism itself rather than as a follow-through adaptation to a revised learner framing; no further tutor turn exists to score as recognition-contingent uptake.
